question_title: What will be the value of "Secured Overnight Financing Rate" on 2026-02-26?
summary: SOFR on Feb 26, 2026 will almost certainly remain in its narrow post-December-cut range of 3.63-3.69%. Key factors: (1) No FOMC meeting before Feb 26 — the next is March 17-18, so the Fed target range stays at 3.50-3.75%. (2) SOFR has been extremely stable since the December cut, with 77% of normal-day readings at 3.64-3.66. (3) Feb 26 is a regular Thursday (second-to-last business day of Feb), with minimal month-end pressure based on historical patterns. Monte Carlo simulation from the empirical distribution of post-cut SOFR values produces a very tight forecast centered on 3.65.

factors.description: No FOMC meeting before Feb 26 (next is March 17-18), so Fed target range remains 3.50-3.75% — SOFR stays anchored in current regime
SOFR extremely stable at 3.63-3.69 on normal days since Dec 2025 cut — 77% of observations at 3.64-3.66
Feb 26 is a regular Thursday, not month-end or quarter-end — minimal repo market calendar effects expected
Slight month-end proximity (1 business day before month-end Feb 27) could push SOFR marginally higher, but Jan 29 (same position) was 3.65 — typical
Small tail risk of repo market disruption pushing SOFR outside normal range, but no known catalysts

reasoning:
Now let me check when the next FOMC meeting is and look at Fed funds futures to understand market expectations.The next FOMC meeting is March 17-18, 2026 — well after the Feb 26 resolution date. So no rate change is expected before resolution. Let me now compute the distribution analytically.**SOFR on Feb 26, 2026 Forecast:**

The forecast is tightly centered around **3.65%**, reflecting SOFR's remarkable stability under the current Fed regime:

- **No rate change coming**: The next FOMC meeting is March 17-18, well after the Feb 26 resolution date. The target range stays at 3.50-3.75%.
- **Empirical stability**: Since the December 2025 rate cut, SOFR has traded in a 3.63-3.69 range on normal (non-month-end) days, with 77% of observations at 3.64-3.66.
- **Calendar effects minimal**: Feb 26 is a Thursday, the second-to-last business day of February. Historical data shows negligible month-end pressure at this position (Jan 29, same relative position, was 3.65).

| Percentile | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| 10th | 3.63 |
| 20th | 3.64 |
| 40th | 3.65 |
| 60th | 3.66 |
| 80th | 3.67 |
| 90th | 3.69 |

The distribution is intentionally very tight — this reflects the empirical reality that SOFR barely moves between FOMC meetings absent repo market stress events.
